behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is core grouping logic (trace_id keying), not an interaction that requires module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; in-memory fixtures reproduce the failure exactly. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: setup mixed entries from svcA/svcB sharing trace_id; assert one group contains both; catches bug where code keys by (service,trace_id). Adjacent Integration candidate: wire parser+grouping; assert same; catches wiring bugs but is not required by this behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text only requires grouping rule "same trace ID ... regardless of which microservice" and does not require cross-module transport/persistence boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test `group_by_trace_id(entries)` with mixed-service fixtures; assert all entries with identical trace_id end up in one group independent of `service_name`.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure likely appears at boundary between skew-compensation logic and timeline ordering/view assembly across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; deterministic in-process data is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: setup pipeline modules (correlator + skew adjuster + timeline builder) with two services offset by 400ms; assert rendered order matches causal sequence; catches handoff bug where adjuster output is ignored by timeline builder. Adjacent Unit candidate: test sorter function only; catches bad comparator but misses module wiring bug where compensation is not applied in timeline view. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is algorithmic/time-normalization ("clock skew of up to 500ms") and does not require real OS clocks or network timing.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test through correlation->skew correction->timeline rendering path using synthetic logs with known skew (<=500ms); assert final timeline order is correct after normalization.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior includes both detection and report flagging, so detector output must propagate into reporting module. REAL-INFRA: no real external infra required; synthetic latency distributions suffice. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: setup baseline calculator + percentile calculator + anomaly reporter with controlled dataset where p99=3.2x baseline; assert anomaly report contains spike flag; catches bug where detection works but report mapping drops flag. Adjacent Unit candidate: test threshold function only; catches math errors but misses detector-to-report handoff failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/load generator/OS effects, only statistical detection and report output.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test over anomaly pipeline using deterministic log latencies to force p99 > 3x baseline; assert anomaly report includes latency-spike flag and correct metric values.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires parser error handling, pipeline resilience, and skipped-count reporting across module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process input stream/collection; no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: setup ingest->parse->process->metrics pipeline with N valid + M malformed lines; assert pipeline completes and reports skipped_count=M; catches bug where exception aborts pipeline or counter not propagated. Adjacent Unit candidate: parser returns error on malformed JSON; catches parse correctness but misses crash/metrics behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real filesystem watcher/socket ingestion; malformed payload handling is testable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test feeding mixed valid/invalid JSON records through full local pipeline; assert no crash, valid records processed, and skipped-entry metric equals malformed count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this is throughput/backpressure behavior tied to real runtime scheduling, buffering, and wall-clock rate; in-process mocks often hide queue saturation and timing regressions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: System candidate: run real tool process, send 10k entries/s for a fixed window, assert processed_count==sent_count and lag/queue depth returns to steady bound; catches real contention/backpressure/drop bugs. Adjacent Integration candidate: call pipeline methods in-process with synthetic loop; can catch gross logic bugs but may miss real process I/O and scheduler-induced drops. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly specifies real-time burst capacity ("10,000 log entries per second" and "without ... falling behind"), which is a runtime/system boundary.
behaviors.test_description: System load test against the real executable/service under burst traffic at 10k logs/s; assert zero drops and bounded end-to-end lag during and after burst.
